componentDescription: Slider is a range input that lets users select a numeric value by dragging a handle along a track. Use for settings with a continuous range (volume, brightness, opacity, price range). For precise numeric entry, use NumberInput instead or alongside the Slider.
features: Configurable min, max, and step values
Visual track with filled portion showing current value

usageExamples.title: Volume control slider
usageExamples.description: Slider for controlling audio volume from 0 to 100.
usageExamples.code:
<Slider
  label="Volume"
  value={volume}
  onChange={setVolume}
  min={0}
  max={100}
  step={1}
/>

usageExamples.title: Price range slider
usageExamples.description: Slider for filtering products by maximum price.

usageExamples.title: Opacity setting with precision step
usageExamples.description: Slider for image opacity with 0.1 precision.
usageExamples.code:
<Slider
  label={`Opacity: ${opacity}`}
  value={opacity}
  onChange={setOpacity}
  min={0}
  max={1}
  step={0.1}
/>

value.llmContext: Required. The current numeric value within the min-max range. Always manage with useState. Initialize to a sensible default (e.g., the midpoint or a recommended setting).

onChange.llmContext: Required. Callback fired with the new numeric value as the user drags. This fires frequently during dragging — avoid expensive operations directly here; use debouncing for API calls.
onChange.propDescription: Required. Called with new numeric value during drag

min.llmContext: The minimum selectable value. Defaults to 0. Set based on the logical lower bound of your range (e.g., 0 for volume, 100 for minimum price).
min.propDescription: Minimum value of the slider range

max.llmContext: The maximum selectable value. Defaults to 100. Set based on the logical upper bound (e.g., 100 for percentage, 1000 for price, 1 for opacity).
max.propDescription: Maximum value of the slider range

step.llmContext: The increment between selectable values. Use 1 for integer values, 0.1 for one decimal place precision, 10 for coarser selections. Smaller steps give more precision but may make fine-tuning difficult.

disabled.llmContext: Set to true to prevent interaction. Use when the slider's controlled feature is unavailable (e.g., audio slider when muted).
